steps.instruction: Add three T flip-flops, three InputSwitches, a Clock, and three LEDs to the canvas
steps.hint: Use the Memory tab to add the T flip-flops and a Clock. Then use the I/O tab to add three InputSwitches and three LEDs.

steps.instruction: Connect each InputSwitch to the T input of a different flip-flop
steps.hint: Turn all three InputSwitches on. A T flip-flop toggles only when its T input is 1.

steps.instruction: Connect the Clock to the Clk input of the first T flip-flop
steps.hint: The first flip-flop receives the external clock signal.

steps.instruction: Connect the Q output of the first flip-flop to the Clk input of the second flip-flop, then connect the Q output of the second flip-flop to the Clk input of the third flip-flop. Each flip-flop is clocked by the output of the previous one, creating a ripple counter
Connect the Q output of each flip-flop to a different LED
steps.hint: Each LED represents one bit of the counter, with the first LED being the least significant bit.

steps.instruction: Turn all three InputSwitches on and start the Clock. Observe the LEDs as the counter runs. The first LED changes state every clock pulse, the second changes every two pulses, and the third changes every four pulses, displaying a repeating 3-bit binary count from 000 to 111
